title: Europe's Energy Troubles: A Story of Conflict and Change
summary: Europe is facing another big energy problem, just like it did a few years ago. Back in 2022, Russia invaded Ukraine, and Europe had to stop buying so much gas from Russia because it was causing high prices and making things hard for people. Leaders like Ursula von der Leyen said Europe needed to find new friends for energy, like the US and Norway. Now, a new conflict in the Middle East is making energy prices go up again. European leaders are worried and trying to find quick fixes, but some people are frustrated because they want long-term plans to make Europe stronger and more independent with energy. Europe has made some changes, like reducing Russian oil imports to only 2%, but it still relies on energy from other places. The article asks if Europe can really secure its own energy for the future.

a fight or war between countries that causes problems
the cost of things, like how much money you pay for gas
people in charge, like presidents or prime ministers who make decisions
being able to do things on your own without needing help from others

options: A conflict in Russia and Ukraine
questions.correct_answer: A conflict in Russia and Ukraine

background_read: Europe is a continent with many countries that work together in groups like the European Union. Energy is important for things like heating, electricity, and making products. Sometimes, conflicts in other parts of the world can affect energy supplies and prices, causing problems for people in Europe. Leaders try to find solutions to keep energy safe and affordable.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Europe is facing another energy crisis due to conflicts
WHERE: In Europe and the Middle East
WHY: To show how conflicts affect energy and if Europe can be independent

perspectives.description: Some people in Europe are upset because they think leaders are not making good long-term plans for energy and keep facing the same problems.
